principles.statement: Use the right Copilot primitive for the job: instructions for ambient context, prompts for reusable entrypoints, agents for role and tool boundaries, and skills for methodology.
Keep always-on instructions short, factual, and narrowly scoped so they survive routing and reduce token drag.
Treat prompt files as thin manual entrypoints and move step-by-step workflow logic into skills or specialist agents.
Judge prompt quality by goals, boundaries, inputs, and success conditions rather than by tone or apparent confidence.
Constrain agents with narrow roles and least-privilege tool access so delegation and review stay legible.
Use public examples to extract durable principles, not to copy prose or assume any one repository is the platform standard.
